findings.description: List formatting is weakened in the HTML conversion.
findings.evidence: On page 1, the PDF clearly presents agenda items I–IV as a structured numbered/roman numeral list with visible indentation and separation. In the HTML, these items appear more like continuous paragraph lines with reduced indentation and weaker visual distinction as list items.

findings.description: Heading hierarchy and emphasis are not fully preserved.
findings.evidence: On page 1, the PDF uses centered, prominent title lines and stronger separation between the meeting title and body. The HTML retains the text but uses a different left-aligned hierarchy with less pronounced visual distinction between the main title block and surrounding content.

findings.description: List formatting is weakened in the HTML conversion.
findings.evidence: On page 2, section V in the PDF is presented as a clearly separated roman numeral heading followed by paragraphs. In the HTML, the section heading and subsequent text are flatter and more paragraph-like, reducing the visual structure.

findings.description: List formatting and section emphasis are reduced.
findings.evidence: On page 3, sections VII–X in the PDF are clearly formatted as roman numeral agenda items with indentation and spacing. In the HTML, these sections are presented more like plain paragraph blocks, making the agenda structure less visually distinct.

findings.description: The HTML includes fabricated boilerplate metadata and styling content not present in the source PDF.
findings.evidence: HTML contains text such as "Converted Document", "Digital Commons NC — Standalone Theme CSS", CSS variables like "--nc-font", and a Google Fonts import. These do not appear anywhere in the PDF pages, which only show the meeting minutes content.

findings.description: Heading hierarchy does not accurately reflect the document structure and nests a second <main> element unnecessarily.
findings.evidence:
HTML uses

MEETING MINUTES

,

November 2, 2023

, and

DRY-CLEANING SOLVENT CLEANUP ACT (DSCA) PROGRAM / STAKEHOLDER WORK GROUP MEETING

. In the PDF, these lines are presented as stacked centered title lines of similar prominence rather than a clear h1/h2 hierarchy. HTML also contains nested
inside
.
